revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''馬見丘陵'''（うまみきゅうりょう）は、[[奈良県]]・[[奈良盆地]]西部にある[[洪積台地]]。[[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]か…」

revision.text:
[[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|[[馬見丘陵公園]]の様子。2010年10月27日撮影。]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = 馬見丘陵 | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|位置}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} '''馬見丘陵'''（うまみきゅうりょう）は、[[奈良盆地]]西部に位置し、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]から同郡[[河合町]]にかけて南北に広がる標高70～80mの[[洪積台地]]から成る[[丘陵]]地帯である。
==概要== 北は[[大和川]]、東は[[高田川 (奈良県)|高田川]]、西は[[葛下川]]でそれぞれ限られる<ref>{{Cite web|title=馬見丘陵とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=コトバンク|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|last=小項目事典,日本大百科全書（ニッポニカ）,世界大百科事典内言及}}</ref>。丘陵内には、国の[[特別史跡]]に指定されている[[巣山古墳]]などをはじめとした[[馬見古墳群]]が分布している。また、[[馬見丘陵公園]]がある。近年では、[[西大和ニュータウン]]など、[[宅地開発]]が盛んとなっている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== *[[馬見古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm 「県営馬見丘陵公園」]（奈良県サイト）
{{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=title}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:奈良県の地形]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うまみきゆうりよう}}

revision.text:
[[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|View of {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}}, taken on October 27, 2010.]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}} to {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}} in the western part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. [[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|View of {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}}, taken on October 27, 2010.]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}} to {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}} in the western part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|ja|巣山古墳|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

revision.text:
[[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|[[馬見丘陵公園]]の様子。2010年10月27日撮影。]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = 馬見丘陵 | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|位置}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}} '''馬見丘陵'''（うまみきゅうりょう）は、[[奈良盆地]]西部に位置し、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]から同郡[[河合町]]にかけて南北に広がる標高70～80mの[[洪積台地]]から成る[[丘陵]]地帯である。
==概要== 北は[[大和川]]、東は[[高田川 (奈良県)|高田川]]、西は[[葛下川]]でそれぞれ限られる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=馬見丘陵とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=コトバンク|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=ブリタニカ国際大百科事典|last=小項目事典,日本大百科全書（ニッポニカ）,世界大百科事典内言及}}</ref>。丘陵内には、国の[[特別史跡]]に指定されている[[巣山古墳]]などをはじめとした[[馬見古墳群]]が分布している。また、[[馬見丘陵公園]]がある。近年では、[[西大和ニュータウン]]など、[[宅地開発]]が盛んとなっている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==関連項目== *[[馬見古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm 「県営馬見丘陵公園」]（奈良県サイト）
{{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E|region:JP-29|display=title}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:日本の丘陵]] [[Category:奈良県の地形]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うまみきゆうりよう}}

'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==
The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|qq=|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|qq=|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>

The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|qq=|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|qq=|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|qq=|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. '''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==
The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.

== Overview ==
The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.

{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.

{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]] to [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]] in the western part of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} [[:en:Suyama Kofun|Suyama Kofun]]. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

[[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|View of {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}, taken on October 27, 2010.]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}} to {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara}} in the western part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Takadagawa River (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historic site|WD=Q1081138}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun}}. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Kyūryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Nishi-Yamato New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Historic site|WD=Q1081138}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Hill Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Q11627542|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.nara.jp/1780.htm "Prefectural Umami Kyūryō Park" (Nara Prefecture website)]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō]][[ja:馬見丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Umami Kyūryō]][[ja:馬見丘陵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:馬見丘陵]]
[[File:馬見丘陵公園にて Umami-kyūryō-kōen - panoramio.jpg|200px|thumb|View of {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Hill Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}, taken on October 27, 2010.]]
{{Location map|Japan Nara Prefecture | lat_deg = 34.0 | lat_min = 33.0 | lat_sec = 39.2 | lon_deg = 135.0 | lon_min = 44.0 | lon_sec = 16.7 | label = Umami Kyūryō | position = bottom | mark = Red Equilateral triangle(R=204,GB=0).svg | marksize =7 | width = 200 | relief = 1 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34.0|33.0|39.2|N|135.0|44.0|16.7|E}}}}
'''Umami Kyūryō''' (馬見丘陵) is a hilly area composed of an alluvial terrace with an elevation of 70–80 meters, stretching north to south from {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}} to {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai, Nara|1=Kawai, Nara}} in the western part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The hills are bordered by the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} to the north, the {{ill|Takadagawa River (Nara)|ja|高田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takadagawa River (Nara)|1=Q11672100|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672100}} to the east, and the {{ill|Katsurage River|ja|葛下川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsurage River|1=Katsuge River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620861}} to the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=What is Umami Kyūryō?|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E8%A6%8B%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5-35130|website=Kotobank|accessdate=2021-02-07|language=ja|first=Britannica International Encyclopedia|last=Small Encyclopedia, Nipponica, World Encyclopedia}}</ref>
Within the hills are the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, including the nationally designated {{ill|Special Historic Site|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Historic site|WD=Q1081138}} {{ill|Suyama Kofun|en|Suyama Kofun|WD=Q11479418|lt=Suyama Kofun|1=Suyama Kofun}}. The area also hosts {{ill|Umami Kyūryō Park|ja|馬見丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kyūryō Park|1=Umami Hill Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668061}}. In recent years, residential developments such as {{ill|Nishi-Yamato New Town|ja|西大和ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Yamato New Town|1=Q11627542|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627542}} have become prominent.
